title: pilosella vaillantii subsp. suomensis
intro: Pilosella vaillantii subsp. suomensis, also known as Finnish mouse-ear hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the daisy family. It has been found to have medicinal properties due to its content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Pilosella vaillantii subsp. suomensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
